terms.definition: An intensive evidence-based therapy improving vocal loudness in Parkinson's disease through high-effort phonation.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient was enrolled in Lee Silverman Voice Treatment to address hypophonia in Parkinson's disease.
terms.contextNotes: Delivered as 16 sessions over four weeks by a certified clinician.

terms.definition: A treatment for non-fluent aphasia using melodic patterns and rhythmic tapping to facilitate speech production.
terms.exampleSentence: Melodic Intonation Therapy was trialled to support functional verbal output in Broca's aphasia.
terms.contextNotes: Best evidence in non-fluent aphasia with relatively preserved comprehension.

terms.definition: Direct intervention targeting the production of specific speech sounds through modelling and structured practice.
terms.exampleSentence: Articulation therapy was provided weekly, focusing on accurate production of /s/ and /z/.
terms.contextNotes: Often follows traditional Van Riper hierarchy from isolation to conversation.

terms.definition: Methods supplementing or replacing speech, including symbol boards, sign and high-tech voice output devices.
terms.exampleSentence: Augmentative and alternative communication was introduced to support functional communication in MND.
terms.contextNotes: Multimodal AAC use is the norm; do not assume a single device meets all needs.

terms.definition: A low-tech AAC display containing symbols, photos or words that the user points to in order to communicate.
terms.exampleSentence: A bedside communication board was provided to support yes/no responses in ICU.
terms.contextNotes: Personalisation increases functional use; involve the family in vocabulary selection.

terms.definition: Picture Exchange Communication System, in which a learner exchanges symbols to request items and comment.
terms.exampleSentence: PECS was introduced for the child with autism to develop functional requesting skills.
terms.contextNotes: Six structured phases, beginning with the physical exchange of a single picture.

terms.definition: A communication programme using signs and symbols alongside speech to support people with learning difficulties.
terms.exampleSentence: Staff training in Makaton was arranged to support the resident's communication needs.
terms.contextNotes: Distinct from BSL; signs are used with spoken English in natural word order.

terms.definition: Targeted movements of the lips, tongue and jaw aimed at improving strength, range and coordination.
terms.exampleSentence: Daily oromotor exercises were prescribed to improve labial seal and lingual strength.
terms.contextNotes: Evidence is strongest when exercises target functional speech or swallow tasks.

terms.definition: Beverages modified to a denser consistency to slow flow and reduce aspiration risk in dysphagia.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient was prescribed Level 2 mildly thick fluids following bedside swallow assessment.
terms.contextNotes: Always specify IDDSI level; review acceptance and hydration regularly.

terms.definition: International Dysphagia Diet Standardisation Initiative providing a global framework for texture-modified diets and fluids.
terms.exampleSentence: Recommend Level 5 minced and moist diet with Level 2 mildly thick fluids per IDDSI.
terms.contextNotes: Levels 0-4 describe fluids; Levels 3-7 describe foods.

terms.definition: A swallowing manoeuvre in which the chin is lowered toward the chest to narrow the airway entrance.
terms.exampleSentence: A chin tuck posture was trialled to reduce penetration on thin fluids.
terms.contextNotes: Effectiveness should be confirmed instrumentally where possible.

terms.definition: A postural strategy in which the head is rotated toward the weaker side to direct bolus down the stronger side.
terms.exampleSentence: A right head turn was effective in clearing left pyriform residue on FEES.
terms.contextNotes: Best evidence in unilateral pharyngeal weakness; confirm with instrumental assessment.

terms.definition: Structured guidance for parents on supporting a child's communication development at home and in daily routines.
terms.exampleSentence: Parent counselling was provided to support modelling of target sounds in everyday play.
terms.contextNotes: Use plain language and check understanding rather than relying on jargon.

terms.definition: Information and skills training provided to family members so they can support a patient's communication or swallowing.
terms.exampleSentence: Family education on safe swallowing strategies was completed prior to discharge after stroke.
terms.contextNotes: Document attendance, content covered and the family's understanding.

terms.definition: A structured patient-centred conversation conveying serious or unwelcome clinical information sensitively.
terms.exampleSentence: Breaking bad news of progressive dysarthria in MND was undertaken jointly with the consultant.
terms.contextNotes: The SPIKES framework is widely used for structuring difficult conversations.

terms.definition: A group of clinicians from different professions collaborating in the assessment and management of a patient.
terms.exampleSentence: SLT contributed to the multidisciplinary team meeting regarding discharge planning post-stroke.
terms.contextNotes: Document SLT recommendations clearly in the shared MDT record.

terms.definition: A formal written document summarising assessment findings, diagnosis, plan and recommendations from the speech and language therapist.
terms.exampleSentence: Please find enclosed the SLT report following dysphagia assessment on the ward.
terms.contextNotes: Include consistencies trialled, response and clear recommendations.

terms.definition: A prescribed period of reduced or absent voice use to allow the vocal folds to recover from injury or strain.
terms.exampleSentence: Two days of complete voice rest were advised post-laryngeal microsurgery.
terms.contextNotes: Specify whether absolute (no voicing) or relative (limited soft voicing).

terms.definition: Lifestyle and behavioural practices that reduce vocal strain and protect the laryngeal tissues.
terms.exampleSentence: Vocal hygiene advice was provided, including hydration and reduction of throat clearing.
terms.contextNotes: Encourage avoidance of whispering, throat clearing and excessive caffeine.

terms.definition: Percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy feeding delivering nutrition directly into the stomach via an abdominal tube.
terms.exampleSentence: PEG feeding was discussed with the family as oral intake became unsafe in advanced MND.
terms.contextNotes: SLT contributes to MDT discussions but does not insert PEGs.

terms.definition: A patient's voluntary agreement to assessment or intervention after being given comprehensible information.
terms.exampleSentence: Informed consent was obtained from the patient prior to videofluoroscopy.
terms.contextNotes: Use accessible communication formats where capacity or comprehension is reduced.

terms.definition: A documented plan outlining safe positioning, diet, fluids and strategies to support a patient at mealtimes.
terms.exampleSentence: A Mealtime Assistance Plan was placed at the bedside detailing IDDSI levels and posture.
terms.contextNotes: Plans should be reviewed whenever swallow status changes.

terms.definition: Techniques used by a partner to facilitate communication with a person with aphasia or cognitive-communication difficulty.
terms.exampleSentence: Nursing staff were trained in supportive communication strategies for the patient with severe aphasia.
terms.contextNotes: Includes writing key words, drawing, gesture and yes/no question framing.

terms.definition: The process of preparing a patient for safe transition from one care setting to another with appropriate supports.
terms.exampleSentence: SLT contributed to discharge planning by providing family training and a written swallow plan.
terms.contextNotes: Ensure community SLT referral and written guidance accompany the patient home.

terms.definition: The delivery of speech and language therapy services remotely using secure video or telephone platforms.
terms.exampleSentence: Follow-up therapy was delivered via telepractice to support access in a remote area.
terms.contextNotes: Confirm consent, suitability and information governance prior to telepractice.
